title: podman --userns=keep-id broken
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

Running rootless "podman run" on RHEL8 has non consistent behavior and seems broken for some container images.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On a RHEL8/Centos8 Machine, run "podman run -it --userns=keep-id  centos:7 /bin/bash"

2. On a RHEL8/Centos8 Machine, run "podman run -it --userns=keep-id  quay.io/ansible/awx-ee:latest /bin/bash"

3. Run "id" inside the bash shells and compare output

**Describe the results you received:**
Run the following as "myuser" on a Centos8 host:
`podman run -it --userns=keep-id  centos:7 /bin/bash
bash-4.2$ id
uid=1214320(myuser) gid=1214320(myuser) groups=1214320(myuser)
`
From the AWX container, run the following as "myuser":
`podman run -it --userns=keep-id  quay.io/ansible/awx-ee:latest /bin/bash
bash-4.4$ id
uid=1000(1000) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
`

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect the same result for both container images, i.e. for the container to use "myuser" inside the container process.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Tue Sep 21 09:41:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Were the Podman commands run on different systems? The results of `keep-id` vary based on the system in use, it makes the running user's UID the UID of the user running podman - so running on different users, with different UIDs, will have different results

From the image config of the Ansible image:
`            "User": "1000",`

I'm betting this is it.
@rhatdan Should User from the image override the default UID/GID set by `--userns=keep-id`?

Yes.
--userns=keep-id should only setup the user namespace, and leave the default be we run as the user who ran the podman command, in the case of no `keep-id`, this would be Your UID but root within the user namespace.  If you run with --userns=keep-id, it should again be your UID. but this time it is mapped to the same UID inside of out of the User Namespace.
If the user specifies --user foobar in either case it should run as the foobar user and if the image has USER foobar, it likewise should run that way.

```
$ cat /tmp/Containerfile 
from fedora 
user bin
 $ podman build /tmp/
STEP 1/2: FROM fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 4545346f2a49 done  
Copying config 3059bef432 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/2: user bin
COMMIT
--> 0bae8b30b4b
0bae8b30b4bc76bb2ed4ba4b11f1f0a25cb7b3db176668578112e47b485d9e9e
$ podman run 0bae8b30b4bc76bb2ed4ba4b11f1f0a25cb7b3db176668578112e47b485d9e9e id
uid=1(bin) gid=1(bin) groups=1(bin)
$ podman run --userns=keep-id 0bae8b30b4bc76bb2ed4ba4b11f1f0a25cb7b3db176668578112e47b485d9e9e id
uid=1(bin) gid=1(bin) groups=1(bin)
```
This seems to work for me.

I don't think we can change the default at this point. Just because a lot of the world has been using this default for a while (toolbox)

You could run your container with --user root and get the behaviour you want.

podman run --user root --userns=keep-id 0bae8b30b4bc76bb2ed4ba4b11f1f0a25cb7b3db176668578112e47b485d9e9e id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
